title: Urinary symptoms
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.
red_flags.rationale: Supports fever, flank pain, haematuria, pregnancy, recurrence, retention, and systemic concern as prompts only.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.

source.title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria

examination.text: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.
examination.rationale: Supports broad LUTS identity and selected male assessment fields.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.

source.title: Lower urinary tract symptoms in men: management

investigations.text: Supports urinalysis, recommendation-qualified urine culture, and renal-function or electrolyte review in qualified pyelonephritis, nausea, or vomiting contexts.
investigations.rationale: Supports clinician-ordered urinalysis and qualified culture documentation.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports urinalysis, recommendation-qualified urine culture, and renal-function or electrolyte review in qualified pyelonephritis, nausea, or vomiting contexts.

source.title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria

investigations.text: Supports acute or chronic urinary-retention context, palpable or percussable bladder, residual volume, creatinine, qualified imaging, and clinician-led follow-up or referral; catheter and medicine instructions are not mapped.
investigations.rationale: Supports retention and bladder-distension context for adult men only.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports acute or chronic urinary-retention context, palpable or percussable bladder, residual volume, creatinine, qualified imaging, and clinician-led follow-up or referral; catheter and medicine instructions are not mapped.

source.title: Lower urinary tract symptoms in men: management

investigations.text: Supports urine dipstick, qualified culture, post-void residual, and bladder-diary documentation in adult women.
investigations.rationale: Supports urine testing and storage-symptom assessment in adult women only.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports urine dipstick, qualified culture, post-void residual, and bladder-diary documentation in adult women.

source.title: Urinary incontinence and pelvic organ prolapse in women: management

medication.text: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.
medication.rationale: Supports selected urinary symptom and context history.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.

source_limitations: The broad triage-style complaint spans infection, storage, obstruction, pregnancy, male, recurrent, and systemic contexts and is not a diagnosis.
Hydration advice, fixed follow-up timing, diagnosis, treatment, medicine, and referral decisions remain unsupported.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
